items.title: Why are the US and Israel framing the ongoing conflict as a religious war?
items.summary: US troops reportedly told the war in Iran is intended to bring about biblical end times, Armageddon.

items.title: An outlier for condemning Israel’s Gaza genocide, Spain says no to Iran war
items.summary: Spain's socialist president is locked in a war of words with Washington, which has threatened to cut trade ties.

items.title: Is the CIA planning to arm Kurdish forces to spark an uprising in Iran?
items.summary: Such a plan could further destabilise Iran rather than promote peace in the country, experts say.

items.title: NATO defences destroy missile fired from Iran over Mediterranean: Turkiye
items.summary: Defence Ministry says missile destroyed as it approached Turkish airspace after crossing Iraq and Syria.

items.title: US submarine sank Iran’s warship off Sri Lanka coast, says Hegseth
items.summary: Sri Lanka says it recovered several bodies and rescued 32 wounded sailors after the frigate sank.

items.title: Drone targets military base in Bahrain
items.summary: Eyewitness video shows a suspected Iranian drone attack on a military base in Bahrain.

items.title: Spanish PM says ‘no to war’ in Middle East
items.summary: Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez said Spain opposes war against Iran and urged the US, Iran and Israel to pursue a resolution.

items.title: Live Updates: Hegseth Says There Will Be No Letup In U.S. Attacks on Iran
items.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the U.S. military campaign against Iran was accelerating, with more warplanes arriving in the region. Earlier, Turkey said NATO had shot down an Iranian missile heading to Turkish airspace.

items.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s Son Emerges as Leading Choice to Be His Successor
items.summary: If Mojtaba Khamenei is chosen by Iran’s senior clerics, it may signal a victory for hard-liners, an analyst said.

items.title: Cuba’s Tourism Industry Is Collapsing as U.S. Moves Deter Travel
items.summary: The Trump administration’s decision to cut off foreign oil to the island is devastating its tourism industry, a key source of income for a government being pushed to the edge.

items.title: After U.S. Attacks on Iran, FIFA Grapples With How to Handle the World Cup
items.summary: Soccer’s global governing body is grappling with a World Cup first: a tournament host going to war with a participating nation.
